body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-17T08:59:07</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>0</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>42</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>15/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>
	<DateofLastSnowfall>14/07/2026</DateofLastSnowfall>
	<SevenDaySnowfall>26</SevenDaySnowfall>
	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>95</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>In Progress</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>4.8</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>4.7</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>8</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Breeze 10-15 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>ESE</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>None</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Excellent</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Sunny</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Sunny. Light winds.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>It's another Stunner!</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p>It'll be another beaut sunny day on the hill.&nbsp; The sky is full of stars and there is just a puff of a breeze from the east.&nbsp; The current temp is a warm 4.8 degrees and we are heading for a balmy top of 8 degrees.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>Although the temp is warm, the humidity has remained very low since yesterday afternoon, which has allowed the snowmakers to have a good run overnight and they are still pumping it out down in the valley this morning.&nbsp; This should make for pretty good turns on firm groomers this morning, and the sunny aspects should soften as the day progresses.&nbsp; Enjoy.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>Heavenly Valley Lift is closed and the area is Not Recommended.&nbsp; &nbsp;The cover is marginal and the surface will remain icy all day on the shaded aspects.&nbsp; The conditions are only suitable for Advanced and Expert skiers and riders, so think about your group's ability before traversing across from the Summit or Road Runner.&nbsp; The Extreme Area is Closed due to an extreme ice hazard.&nbsp; Do Not Enter!</p><p>Cheers Bill</p><p><br></p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
    <LiftsOpen>0</LiftsOpen>
    <LiftsStandby>0</LiftsStandby>
    <LiftsClosed>0</LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
    <TerrainParks>3</TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>The cover is marginal and there is a significant slide hazard in the backcountry.   The sunniest of aspects may soften for spring-like turns as the day warms but shaded and south facing slopes will likely stay very slick and dangerous for the day.  Take care. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
